title: Sinaloa Cartel Affiliate Who Smuggled 'Busloads' of Drugs Into Georgia Sentenced to 15+ Years
detail: José Favela, a Sinaloa Cartel associate who smuggled methamphetamine and other drugs into Lamar County, Georgia by the busload, was sentenced to over 15 years in federal prison by U.S. District Judge Tillman Self in the Middle District of Georgia on March 27, 2026. Favela was convicted of methamphetamine distribution conspiracy. The case illustrates the Sinaloa Cartel's extensive US distribution infrastructure, which persists despite the ongoing Chapitos-Mayos civil war. Prosecutors noted that Sinaloa-affiliated networks continue to move product northward even as leadership instability disrupts production in Culiacán.

sources.name: US Department of Justice / Middle District of Georgia

media.caption: Sinaloa Cartel affiliate sentenced to 15+ years for smuggling drugs into Georgia
